content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

September 26, 1984

Mike Flick
P.O. Box 2810
Vista, CA 92083

Re: Telephone Advice, Our Advice
No. A-84-243

Dear Mr. Flick:

This letter is sent to confirm a telephone conversation that
you had with Janis Shank McLean of this office.

FACTS

You are presently employed as a staff member to the
San Diego County Board of Supervisors, and you are running for
election as Mayor of the City of Carlsbad.

QUESTIONS PRESENTED

1. Does the Political Reform Act prevent you from assuming
the position of Mayor of the City of Carlsbad?

2. If you are elected, will you be required to disqualify
yourself in your capacity as Mayor from decisions affecting the
County of San Diego or, conversely, in your capacity as an aide
to the Board of Supervisors, from decisions affecting the City
of Carlsbad?

CONCLUSIONS

1. The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from
assuming the position of Mayor of the City of Carlsbad.

2. If you are elected, you will not be required to
disqualify yourself from decisions affecting the County of
San Diego nor from decisions affecting the City of Carlsbad.

Mike Flick
September 26, 1984
Page 2


DISCUSSION

The Political Reform Act does not prevent you from serving
as Mayor of the City of Carlsbad. However, the Act may affect
your governmental decisionmaking. Government Code Section
87100¹/ prohibits a public official from making, participating
in the making, or in any way attempting to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows
or has reason to know he has a financial interest. An official
has a "financial interest" in a decision within the meaning of
Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have a material financial effect on:

     (c) Any source of income ... aggregating $250 or more
in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

Section 87103(c).

However, the County of San Diego (and, conversely, the City
of Carlsbad if you are elected) are not sources of income to you
within the meaning of Section 87103.2/ Under the Political
Reform Act, salary received from a local government agency is
not considered to be "income." See Section 82030(b)(2).
Accordingly, you would not be required to disqualify yourself
from decisions which would materially affect the County or the
City.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at 916/322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Counsel, Legal Division

DMF:km

_____________

1/ All statutory references made are to the Government
Code.

2/ I am assuming that you receive a salary from the
County and would receive compensation from the City if you are
elected.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012


July 25, 1984


Mr. R. Michael Flick
1630 Alta Vista Drive
Vista, CA 92083

Dear Mr. Flick:

I am in receipt of your letter dated July 19, 1984
concerning your filing obligations under the Political
Reform Act.

There are several questions which I need to ask in
order to determine whether or not any amendments need to
be filed. I have been unable to contact you by telephone.
Please call (collect is OK) or drop me a note with a tele-
phone number where I can reach you during the day.


Sincerely,

[signature]
HELEN P. ARRIOLA
Political Reform Consultant

HPA:cv

[illegible handwritten notes at bottom]
